parts.answer: 5 values of I all ascending and to 2 decimal places at least 1 • axes labelled with quantity and unit • appropriate scales (plots occupying at least ½ grid) • plots all correct to ½ small square and precise plots • well-judged line and thin line and triangle method seen on graph R in range 4.0 () to 10.0 () uncertain connection point on crocodile clip / resistance wire irregular / curved / not uniform / differing thickness along wire reduce e.m.f. of power supply / thinner resistance wire / use of additional series / protective resistor to reduce current October/November 2024 Question Answer Marks  = 15  1

pin separation ⩾ 5.0 cm 1st set of lines present and in correct positions 1 n in range 1.30 to 1.70 from correct calculation 1 α = 25  2 justification matching statement e.g. ‘within limits of experimental accuracy’ / owtte with values to support 1 thin lines / use sharp pencil, look at base of pins/keep pins vertical, thin pins October/November 2024 Question Answer Marks 4 MP1 apparatus: stopwatch and metre ruler and protractor MP2 method: measure independent variable measure distance and time between points repeat for different angle MP3 source of inaccuracy (one from): difficult to time at exact point, ramp surface not regular, trolley might travel at an angle across slope MP4 control variable (one from): distance from start to first point, distance between points MP5 table: columns for independent variable, time and speed, all with units MP6 analysis: compare readings of angle with speed or time plot line graph (with axes specified) MP7 additional point (one from): at least 5 sets of data taken, repeat each measurement and take average, 2nd control variable stated
